folio.label: 107v
folio.l: She seitú nat ones nay , whan she seiú ©e
Do this seitú he , al redy Sire seitú she
O blisseful ordre of wedlak , preciouse
Thow that art so mery , and eke so vertuouse
And so comended , and approued eke
That ony man , æ holditú hi~ wortú a leke
Vp oon his bare knees , ougút al his lyf
Thanke his god , that hi~ hatú sent a wyf
Or ellis preye to god , hym for to sende
A wyf , to laste , vn to his lyues ende
For than his lyf , is sette in sekernes
He may nat be , disseyued as I gesse
So that he werke , after his wyues rede
Than may he boldely , bere vp his hede
They ben so trewe , and ther witú al so wyse
For whicú , if æè wylt werke , as the wyse
Do alwey , so as wommen wol the rede
Lo how æat Iacob , as theise Clerkes rede
By good counseil , of his moder rebekke
Bond the kydes skyn , a boute his nekke
For whicú , his fadres benysou~ he wan
Lo Iudytú , as the story ekeeke telle can
Be wys counseil , she goddis peeple kepte
And slougú hi~ , Olofernë , while he slepte
Lo AbygaÂ , by good counseil , how she
Saued hire husbonde , Nabal , whan that he
Shulde haue be slayn , and loke Ester also
By good counseil , delyuered out of woo
The peeple of god , and mad hi~ Mardochee
Of assureassuerassuere , enhaunced for to be
There nys no thyngö , in gree su¹latyf
As seitú Senek , a boue an humble wyf
Suffre thy wyues tonge , as Catou~ byt
She shal comaunde , and æè shalt suffren it
And ©et she wyl obeye , of curteisye
A wyf is kepere , of thyn husbondrye
Wel may the Syke man , bywaylle and wepe
There as nys no wyf , the hous to kepe
I warne the , if wysely æè wolt werche
Loue wel thy wyf , as cést loued his chirche
If æè loue thy self , thow louest thy wyf
Noman hatetú his flessú , but in his lyf
He fostretú it , and therfore bidde I the
Chere thy wyf , or thow shalt neuäe thee
Husbonde or wyf , what so men iape or pleye
Of worldly folk , holden the sekyr weye
